title: Xcode debug adapter configuration
description: Scenario config for the `Xcode` adapter (ZedXcode). Mirrors `LaunchConfig` in crates/xcode-dap-config; consumed by the `xcode-dap` proxy as the DAP `launch` request arguments.

workspace.description: Path to the .xcworkspace (or .xcodeproj) to build, e.g. "$ZED_WORKTREE_ROOT/MyApp.xcworkspace". Zed task variables are substituted before launch.

scheme.description: Xcode scheme to build and run, e.g. "MyApp".

device.description: Simulator device name (e.g. "iPhone 15 Pro Max") or UDID. A booted simulator matching it is preferred. If omitted, the booted iPhone simulator is used, else the newest available iPhone.

os.description: Simulator OS version to narrow device matching, e.g. "26.3". Defaults to the newest runtime available for the device.

configuration.description: Build configuration, e.g. "Debug" or "Release". Defaults to the scheme's default configuration.

preflight.description: Shell command run before the build when the workspace file is missing (project generation), e.g. "make project CI=true".

oslog.description: Also pump OSLog output (`log stream --predicate`) into the Debug Console, in addition to the app's stdout/stderr. By default only the app's own logging is shown (subsystem == bundle id, or any binary image inside the .app bundle); set `oslogPredicate` to override.

oslogPredicate.description: Custom NSPredicate for the OSLog pump, passed to `log stream --predicate`, e.g. "subsystem == \"com.example.app\"". Overrides the default app-scoped predicate. Only used when `oslog` is true.

terminateOnStop.description: Terminate the app on the simulator when the debug session stops (Xcode Stop semantics).

buildOutput.description: Build-log verbosity in the Debug Console: "filtered" shows phase headers, diagnostics and the final verdict; "full" streams the entire xcodebuild log.

verboseLogging.description: Log this debug session at trace verbosity (DAP frame summaries and bodies, engine commands) to ~/.zedxcode/logs/xcode-dap.log. Takes effect at the launch request (which is replayed into the log); frames before launch (e.g. initialize) are only captured with the XCODE_DAP_LOG environment variable. Never lowers a level already raised via XCODE_DAP_LOG.

derivedData.description: Explicit DerivedData directory, mapped to `xcodebuild -derivedDataPath`. Defaults to xcodebuild's per-workspace DerivedData location when omitted.
